front: Define eigenvalue and eigenvector of a linear transformation T
back: A scalar λ is an eigenvalue of T if there exists a non-zero vector v such that T(v) = λv. Such a non-zero vector v is called an eigenvector corresponding to λ.

front: What is the characteristic polynomial of a matrix A?
back: The characteristic polynomial is det(A - λI) = 0, which is a polynomial in λ whose roots give the eigenvalues of A.

front: Define eigenspace corresponding to an eigenvalue λ
back: The eigenspace Eλ is the set of all eigenvectors corresponding to λ, plus the zero vector. Mathematically, Eλ = Null(A - λI).

front: What is the geometric multiplicity of an eigenvalue?
back: Geometric multiplicity is the dimension of the eigenspace, i.e., the number of linearly independent eigenvectors corresponding to that eigenvalue.

front: What is the algebraic multiplicity of an eigenvalue?
back: Algebraic multiplicity is the number of times an eigenvalue appears as a root of the characteristic polynomial.
Every square matrix satisfies its own characteristic equation; i.e., if p(λ) = det(A - λI), then p(A) = 0.

front: When is a matrix A diagonalizable?
back: A matrix A is diagonalizable if it has n linearly independent eigenvectors (for an n×n matrix), i.e., the sum of geometric multiplicities equals n.
Two matrices A and B are similar if B = P⁻¹AP for some invertible matrix P. Similar matrices represent the same linear transformation in different bases.

front: Define the minimal polynomial of a matrix
back: The minimal polynomial m(λ) is the monic polynomial of least degree such that m(A) = 0. It divides the characteristic polynomial.
A matrix N is nilpotent if N^k = 0 for some positive integer k. All eigenvalues of a nilpotent matrix are 0.
A matrix A is diagonalizable as A = PDP⁻¹ where D is a diagonal matrix containing eigenvalues, and P contains corresponding eigenvectors as columns.

front: What is the spectrum of a matrix?
back: The spectrum is the set of all eigenvalues of a matrix, often denoted as σ(A).
A matrix is defective if it does not have a complete set of linearly independent eigenvectors (not diagonalizable).

front: What is the rank of eigenspace?
back: The rank of eigenspace equals its geometric multiplicity, which is the dimension of Null(A - λI).
